channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [7] Taking snapshots of spin–valley modes in a moiré superlattice
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 14 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03295-3

An ultrafast imaging technique captured the propagation of charge-decoupled excitations in twisted bilayer WSe2. Two spin–valley modes with distinct propagation behaviours were revealed, consistent with the phase and amplitude modes of a spin–valley superfluid.
item.dc:title: [7] Taking snapshots of spin–valley modes in a moiré superlattice
item.description:
Authors: Authors not available in source RSS

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Ultrafast imaging of charge-decoupled spin-valley modes in a twisted bilayer WSe2 moiré superlattice.

Tags: #moire #WSe2 #spin-valley

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 14 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03295-3 An ultrafast imaging technique captured the propagation of charge-decoupled excitations in twisted bilayer WSe2. Two spin–valley modes with distinct propagation behaviours were revealed, consistent with the phase and amplitude modes of a spin–valley superfluid.

item.title: [7] Observation of angular momentum transfer among crystal lattice modes
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03274-8

How angular momentum is exchanged and conserved among lattice modes has been difficult to measure experimentally, but has now been observed via a coherent three-phonon scattering process in a topological insulator.
item.dc:title: [7] Observation of angular momentum transfer among crystal lattice modes
item.dc:creator: Olga Minakova, Carolina Paiva, Maximilian Frenzel, et al.
Carolina Paiva
Maximilian Frenzel
Michael S. Spencer
Joanna M. Urban
Christoph Ringkamp
Martin Wolf
Gregor Mussler
Dominik M. Juraschek
Sebastian F. Maehrlein
item.description:
Last authors: Dominik M. Juraschek, Sebastian F. Maehrlein

Authors: Olga Minakova, Carolina Paiva, Maximilian Frenzel, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of lattice angular momentum transfer via phonon scattering in a topological insulator.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #phonons #latticedynamics

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03274-8 How angular momentum is exchanged and conserved among lattice modes has been difficult to measure experimentally, but has now been observed via a coherent three-phonon scattering process in a topological insulator.

item.title: [9] Correlated insulator in the kagome flat band of a two-dimensional electrostatic crystal
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03291-7

A tunable artificial crystal in a shallow GaAs quantum well is shown to enable interaction-driven insulating behaviour. Electrostatic control tunes the band structure from graphene-like to kagome-like bands.
item.dc:title: [9] Correlated insulator in the kagome flat band of a two-dimensional electrostatic crystal
item.dc:creator: Daisy Q. Wang, Zeb Krix, Olga A. Tkachenko, et al.
Zeb Krix
Olga A. Tkachenko
Vitaly A. Tkachenko
Chong Chen
Ian Farrer
David A. Ritchie
Oleg P. Sushkov
Alexander R. Hamilton
Oleh Klochan
item.description:
Last authors: Alexander R. Hamilton, Oleh Klochan

Authors: Daisy Q. Wang, Zeb Krix, Olga A. Tkachenko, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Observation of correlated states and flat-band physics in an artificial kagome lattice system.

Tags: #kagome #flat-band #correlated-insulator

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 11 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03291-7 A tunable artificial crystal in a shallow GaAs quantum well is shown to enable interaction-driven insulating behaviour. Electrostatic control tunes the band structure from graphene-like to kagome-like bands.
